author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XXIII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A psalm of David himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Church speaks to Christ: “The Lord feedeth me, and I shall lack nothing” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord Jesus Christ is my Shepherd, “and I shall lack nothing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In a place of pasture there hath He placed me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In a place of fresh pasture, leading me to faith, there hath He placed me to be nourished.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “By the water of refreshing hath He brought me up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By the water of baptism, whereby they are refreshed who have lost health and strength, hath He brought me up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath converted my soul: He hath led me forth in the paths of righteousness, for His Name’s sake” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath brought me forth in the narrow ways, wherein few walk, of His righteousness; not for my merit’s sake, but for His Name’s sake.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Yea, though I walk in the midst of the shadow of death” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yea, though I walk in the midst of this life, which is the shadow of death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will fear no evil, for Thou art with me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will fear no evil, for Thou dwellest in my heart by faith: and Thou art now with me, that after the shadow of death I too may be with Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy rod and Thy staff, they have comforted me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy discipline, like a rod for a flock of sheep, and like a staff for children of some size, and growing out of the natural into spiritual life, they have not been grievous to me; rather have they comforted me: because Thou art mindful of me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast prepared a table in my sight, against them that trouble me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now after the rod, whereby, whilst a little one, and living the natural life, I was brought up among the flock in the pastures; after that rod, I say, when I began to be under the staff, Thou hast prepared a table in my sight, that I should no more be fed as a babe with milk, but being older should take meat, strengthened against them that trouble me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast fattened my head with oil.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast gladdened my mind with spiritual joy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Thy inebriating cup, how excellent is it!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And Thy cup yielding forgetfulness of former vain delights, how excellent is it!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Thy mercy shall follow me all the days of my life:” that is, as long as I live in this mortal life, not Thine, but mine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That I may dwell in the house of the Lord for length of days” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now Thy mercy shall follow me not here only, but also that I may dwell in the house of the Lord for ever.
